name: Newly-Weds Chocolate Chip Roll
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to the highest position and preheat to 375°F (190°C).
2. Grease a jelly roll pan, 15 1/2 x 10 1/2 inches, and line with waxed paper.
3. In a large mixing bowl, combine egg whites and salt; beat until foamy.
4. Gradually add 1/4 cup sugar and vanilla extract to the egg whites, beating until stiff but not dry.
5. In a small bowl, beat egg yolks with the remaining sugar until very thick and lemon-colored (about 5-10 minutes).
6. Sift together 1/4 cup cocoa powder, flour, and cornstarch into the egg yolk mixture.
7. Gently fold the sifted dry ingredients into the beaten egg yolks.
8. Gradually fold this mixture into the beaten egg whites.
9. Pour the batter evenly into the prepared pan.
10. Bake for 12-15 minutes until lightly browned and springy to the touch.
11. Meanwhile, sprinkle a towel with confectioner's sugar.
12. Invert the cake onto the sugar-dusted towel and peel away the waxed paper.
13. While still hot, roll the cake up in two jelly-roll fashion from the long side.
14. Let it stand for 1 minute, then unroll to allow steam to escape before re-rolling with the towel.
15. Cool completely on a wire rack.
16. Unroll and spread with softened ice cream.
17. Re-roll, cover, and freeze until firm.
18. Sprinkle with 1 teaspoon cocoa powder just before serving.
